articles.title: India growth expected to be around 6.5% this year: Gita Gopinath
articles.description: India's economy is projected to grow at 6.5% this year. Global risks persist, impacting supply chains and potentially leading to food price increases. Companies are absorbing costs, but this may not last. Central banks might delay rate cuts due to muted global demand. Any oil shock will affect many sectors.

articles.title: Ashoka Buildcon secures power sector related project in Angola
articles.description: Ashoka Buildcon announced that the company had submitted bid to the Ministry of Energy and Water, the Republic of Angola, for the project viz. 'Electricity Sector Improvement and Access Project'.

articles.title: Barometers trade with deep cuts; Nifty trades below 23,900
articles.description: The Nifty traded below the 23,900 level. All the sectoral indices on the NSE were traded in the red, with IT, media and pharma shares declining the most.

articles.title: Pani puri vendor sells cart for wife's hospital bill, customer invests Rs 1.5 lakh for fresh start
articles.description: A pani puri vendor sold his cart to cover his wife's hospital bills, leaving him with nothing. One loyal customer tracked him down, skipped the sympathy, and handed him a Rs 1.5 lakh business plan.

articles.title: 'May not be sufficient': FM Sitharaman sounds alarm on AI threats to Indian banks
articles.description: Finance Minister flags that decades of strong cybersecurity may be insufficient against AI-driven threats, directs banks to work under an IBA-led mechanism to identify vulnerabilities and invest in new defences.

articles.description: Chennai Petro's consolidated revenue for Q4 rose 7.23% quarter-on-quarter to Rs 16,817 crore, from Rs 15,683 crore in the previous quarter.

articles.title: Economic growth, prosperity remain primary impulse for changes in India: India's Ambassador to the US Vinay Mohan Kwatra
articles.description: India's Ambassador to the US, Vinay Mohan Kwatra, highlighted economic growth as India's primary driver for change, expressing concern over a global "control regime" trend. He detailed India's "7 per cent-plus GDP growth" alongside governance reforms and infrastructure development, including digital advancements, as key transformations.

articles.title: Real estate deals down 63% to $763 million in Jan-Mar against Dec quarter: Report
articles.description: India's real estate sector saw a significant 63 percent drop in deal value during January-March 2026. This decline to USD 763 million occurred because of fewer large-ticket transactions. The period recorded strong deal volumes with 32 transactions, but the overall value was impacted. Private equity deals and merger and acquisition deals also saw a decrease.

articles.title: RBI a net buyer of US dollars in February
articles.description: The Reserve Bank of India became a net buyer of $7.4 billion in February, a shift from January's net sales. This likely benefited from a stronger rupee after the India-US trade deal announcement. The real effective exchange rate (REER) for the rupee decreased in March, indicating undervaluation and supporting export competitiveness.

articles.title: Cisco shows switch that can connect quantum computers of different kinds

articles.title: SP Group to raise Rs 25,400 crore via bonds, loans
articles.description: Shapoorji Pallonji Group is nearing the closure of a ₹25,400 crore debt raise by May 15, featuring annual coupons around 18.75%. This fundraising, part of Project Ascent, will utilize USD bonds and bespoke loans to refinance existing debt, including bonds secured against its Tata Sons stake.

articles.title: Panic buying, Nepal price gap fuel demand surge in UP border districts
articles.description: Officials said the spike in border districts is partly driven by petrol being taken across to Nepal, where prices are around ₹60 per litre higher. Authorities are closely monitoring the situation amid concerns of diversion, even as rumours of a post-election fuel price hike have added to public anxiety. Social media speculation around rising global crude oil prices due to geopolitical tensions, despite stable domestic rates, has further fuelled panic, though officials insist no price revision has been announced.

articles.title: Yuan to top Yen in currency options trading: LCH
articles.description: The yuan is poised to surpass the yen as the second-most traded currency against the dollar in foreign-exchange options, according to LCH. This shift, expected by 2028, reflects growing yuan internationalization and market demand for currency diversification beyond the dollar.

articles.title: These large-, mid- and small-cap stocks can give more than 22% return in 1 year, according to analysts
articles.description: When a market is in a secular correction due to fears that the economy might take a hit for some reason, the fall is generally across the board. The question at this point of time: Is the Indian market at the beginning of a second phase of such a correction? The answer is not as clear as it should be, because of several reasons.

articles.title: Regeneron and Trump Strike a Deal: What It Means for Drug Prices In US
articles.description: Regeneron struck a drug pricing deal with the Trump administration on Thursday, agreeing to lower costs for some American patients as part of the president's "most favored nation" push. The agreement, which shields Regeneron from pharmaceutical tariffs for three years, makes it the 17th drugmaker to reach such a deal with the White House.

articles.title: Amazon is replacing traditional titles in Ring and Blink units, memo says: ‘As Builders, we define and ​reward success through...’
articles.description: Amazon is ditching traditional job titles like 'senior' and 'lead' for product teams at Ring and Blink, rebranding employees as 'builders' and managers as 'builder leaders'. This move aims to reduce hierarchy and focus on customer value creation. While concerns about career progression exist, Amazon assures compensation and growth paths remain unaffected, fostering a culture of experimentation.

articles.title: LTM Q4FY26 results: Net profit rises 23% to ₹1,392.3 cr, revenue up 15.55%
articles.description: IT major LTM (formerly LTIMindtree) on Thursday reported a 23.37 per cent rise in consolidated net profit to Rs 1,392.3 crore in the January-March quarter of FY26. The company, a part of the Larsen & Toubro (L&T) Group, had posted a net profit (attributable to shareholders of the company) of Rs 1,128.5 crore in the same period of the preceding fiscal. The company's revenue from operations saw a 15.55 per cent increase to Rs 11,291.7 crore in Q4 FY26, as compared to Rs 9,771.7 crore in Q4 FY25. On a quarter-on-quarter basis, profit and revenue climbed 43.44 per cent and 4.73 per cent, respectively. For the full fiscal year of 2025-26, the company's profit was 9.12 per cent higher at Rs 5,018.1 crore, while revenue from operations was up 11.31 per cent at Rs 42,307.6 crore. "In FY26, we accelerated our strategic shift to an AI-centric organisation with the intelligence of the BlueVerse platform and talent transformation at scale. Over the year, we unlocked new levels of ...
